To derive the total query response time function $ T_{\text{Total}}(Q) $ we must sum the individual response times of each database. The query distribution is given as follows: MySQL handles $ \alpha Q $ queries Oracle handles $ \beta Q $ queries and Sybase handles $ (1 - \alpha - \beta)Q $ queries.

The total query response time is the sum of the response times for each database. Substituting the given functions into this model we get the total response time function.

The total query response time function $ T_{\text{Total}}(Q) $ is the sum of the response times of the three databases. This is calculated by substituting the number of queries each database handles into their respective response time functions.

The total query response time function $ T_{\text{Total}}(Q) $ is defined as the sum of the response times of MySQL Oracle and Sybase. This function represents the combined performance of all three databases under the given distribution of queries.

Using the given constants $ a = 0.01 $ $ b = 0.005 $ $ c = 0.02 $ $ d = 0.03 $ $ e = 2 $ $ f = 0.04 $ and $ g = 0.01 $ and the total number of queries $ Q = 1000 $ we can now calculate the total response time for different values of $ \alpha $ and $ \beta $.

The total query response time function $ T_{\text{Total}}(Q) $ can be minimized by finding the values of $ \alpha $ and $ \beta $ that result in the lowest total response time. This involves evaluating the function for various combinations of $ \alpha $ and $ \beta $ and selecting the pair that yields the minimum value.

To find the optimal values of $ \alpha $ and $ \beta $ that minimize the total query response time we can use numerical optimization techniques. This involves evaluating the function over a range of possible values for $ \alpha $ and $ \beta $ and identifying the combination that gives the lowest total response time.

The optimal values of $ \alpha $ and $ \beta $ are determined by minimizing the total query response time function. This is achieved by systematically testing different values of $ \alpha $ and $ \beta $ and calculating the corresponding total response time for each combination.
